text:
In a little town called Willowridge, there lived an owl named Olive. She loved mysteries. One quiet evening, she decided to solve the mystery of why butterflies seemed fearful.

She watched from her nest high up in an old elm tree. Something big shuffled by. Owl ears perkied. A hawk!

But no.

Just fleays.

Could snakes love berries?

That sounded silly.

Maybe they wanted her eggs.

Olive soared down. There were heaps!

She took an oober.

Why shouldn moth?

Because it fluttered.

You flutter.

I flutter?

No moth. Owls don't eat moths. Maybe you like branches.

She clucked.

She liked flowers.

Flowers don't cling.

Maybe you love my flowers.

Did I?

Olive. You intrigue me!

She plucked one.

You lovely.

I won't eat you.

She took flight.

That's kind.


I just love secrets.



Sometimes she imagined them singing.

But mostly secrets.




She'd sleep.
